tasks.title: Reviewed dataset inventory and larger-volume ingestion
tasks.summary: Add reviewed dataset inventory, support compressed reviewed datasets, and expose the inventory over HTTP/UI.

tasks.title: Expand public EMAIL and TICKET validation slices
tasks.summary: Add more support-representative medium and long email/ticket public records and keep downstream pipeline tests green.

tasks.title: Queue and transcript-length validation dashboarding
tasks.summary: Emit queue and transcript-length scoped validation alerts and surface the breakdowns in the self-hosted console.

tasks.title: Production reviewed-export pipeline hardening
tasks.summary: Reviewed-export refresh now carries manifest policy metadata, coverage failures, snapshot pruning, and deployment guidance for secure scheduled use.
acceptance: Reviewed export refresh supports an ops-friendly production mode.
Integrity or coverage checks are recorded for refreshed datasets.
Docs explain how to run the scheduled export path safely outside local dev.

tasks.title: External observability for model validation
tasks.summary: Prometheus-facing reviewed-dataset and reviewed-export health gauges now complement validation drift metrics, and docs describe the required dashboards and alerts.
acceptance: Validation counters and gauges are exported for external scraping.
Queue and transcript-length regressions are visible from metrics, not only the built-in UI.
Docs explain how to wire dashboards and alerts.

tasks.title: Real tenant shadow-validation readiness
tasks.summary: Nightly validation now deduplicates larger reviewed-export trees, enforces dataset readiness floors, and surfaces coverage/freshness gaps before reports run.
acceptance: Nightly validation handles larger reviewed-export trees safely.
Coverage summaries make under-sampled queues and lengths obvious.
Docs spell out the production shadow-validation procedure.

tasks.title: Security and retention hardening
tasks.summary: Reviewed-export retention, transcript-inclusion policy, classification metadata, and operator guidance are now configurable for self-hosted and embedded installs.
acceptance: Retention behavior is configurable and documented.
Reviewed-export handling has explicit safety guidance.
Docs reflect the security posture for self-hosted installs.

tasks.title: Public engagement benchmark loop
tasks.summary: Run the isolated public call/email/ticket benchmark loop against local Ollama, persist baseline vs calibrated artifacts, add worst-record reporting, and document the current quality gaps.
acceptance: The benchmark runner is exercised against the public engagement suite.
Artifacts make the worst engagement and queue gaps obvious.
Docs explain how to run the benchmark loop repeatedly during tuning.

tasks.title: Benchmark trial-matrix stability loop
tasks.summary: Completed on March 29, 2026: repeated isolated large-suite trials now emit best/median/worst artifacts, and the tuned local Ollama qwen3.5 loop is deterministic on score quality across three runs.
acceptance: The loop can run multiple isolated benchmark trials into one artifact tree.
Aggregate artifacts report best, median, and worst calibrated runs.
Docs explain when to use repeated trials for provider-compatibility validation.

tasks.title: Reviewed export benchmark loop
tasks.summary: A reviewed-export benchmark loop now builds suites from secure dataset trees, writes dataset summaries, and runs baseline vs calibrated loops in isolated or in-process mode.
acceptance: The loop supports reviewed-export trees with transcripts and analyst labels.
Calibrated vs baseline comparisons are written for each run.
Operator guidance explains how to point the loop at secure reviewed datasets.

tasks.title: Engagement-specific quality gate closure
tasks.summary: Completed on March 29, 2026 for the current public engagement suite: CALL, EMAIL, and TICKET slices now pass the large-suite quality gates locally with explicit loop artifacts and stable repeated-trial evidence.
acceptance: Loop reports include pass/fail gate summaries for overall and engagement-specific slices.
Worst-performing engagement buckets are easy to identify from the report artifacts.
Docs state the current benchmark thresholds and how to interpret them.
